title: 3 Hot Longevity Startups Selling Health and Hope: Retro Biosciences, NewLimit, Insilico
description: In-depth profiles of Retro Biosciences, NewLimit, and Insilico Medicine -- three startups at the forefront of age reversal, epigenetic reprogramming, and AI-driven drug discovery for longevity.

title: Vitamin C Identified as Direct ACSL4 Inhibitor That Alleviates Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Landmark study defines 'ferro-aging' -- an iron-lipid axis driving primate aging. Long-term vitamin C administration in aged monkeys reversed biological age markers and reduced multi-organ pathology via ACSL4 inhibition.

title: Steve Horvath: March 2026 Turning Into a Good Month for Longevity Supplement Research
description: Epigenetic clock pioneer Steve Horvath highlights the convergence of positive longevity supplement findings: COSMOS multivitamin trial and the new vitamin C ferro-aging primate study.

title: Advisory Board: Multivitamin Could Help Slow Biological Aging
description: Analysis of the COSMOS trial results, noting that Columbia researchers call the findings a 'major advance for the supplement field' while emphasizing uncertainties about epigenetic clock interpretation.

title: Rapamycin Dosing for Longevity: What Emerging Human Research Reveals
description: Comprehensive analysis suggesting a plausible human longevity dose of 5-10mg/week, drawing on PEARL trial data, 2025 IVF rapamycin trial, and animal studies to frame optimal mTORC1 inhibition strategy.

title: Senolytics for Aging 2026: Dasatinib, Quercetin, and Fisetin Protocols
description: Updated guide to senolytic therapy in 2026, covering D+Q and fisetin protocols, Mayo Clinic pulmonary fibrosis trial results, and practical dosing strategies for clearing senescent cells.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising in 2026
description: The five most-funded longevity startups (Altos Labs, Oura, Retro Biosciences, BioSplice, Insilico Medicine) account for roughly $6.9 billion, with the top 10 capturing 67% of all sector funding.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033
description: Market analysis projects the longevity biotech sector will grow at a 20.4% CAGR from 2026 to 2033, driven by AI integration, genomics, and regenerative medicine advances.

title: COSMOS Trial: Daily Multivitamin Slows Biological Aging by ~4 Months Over 2 Years
description: A large randomized trial published in Nature Medicine found that a daily multivitamin slowed epigenetic aging by about four months over two years, with greater benefits for the biologically older.

title: ARPA-H Devotes $144 Million to Studies That Slow Aging and Extend Quality of Life
description: The PROSPR program awarded $144M to seven teams to build the framework for phase 3 clinical trials of interventions intended to increase human healthspan, including rapamycin and semaglutide.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Profiles of 11 key companies including Altos Labs ($3B), Life Biosciences, NewLimit, and Genflow Biosciences, covering epigenetic reprogramming, gene therapy, and senolytic approaches.

title: WHOOP Joins $34.5M ARPA-H-Backed Coalition for First FDA-Grade Aging Score
description: WHOOP joins Stanford-led THRIVE coalition to develop an FDA-grade 'Intrinsic Capacity' score that predicts major health outcomes up to 20 years in advance using wearable and biomarker data.

title: Eric Verdin (Buck Institute) on CD38 Inhibitors Extending Mouse Lifespan
description: Buck Institute president Eric Verdin presents data on CD38 inhibitor small molecules that correct NAD+ decline and extend lifespan in mouse models.

title: Anti-Aging Therapy Market Eyes $420B Milestone; Klotho, NAD+, and Gene Editing Advance
description: The anti-aging market could surpass $420B by 2030. Avai Bio begins Klotho master cell bank production, Niagen expands NAD+ patents, and Intellia's CRISPR trial resumes after FDA lift.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytic and NK Cell Advances
description: Monthly roundup highlights research in Nature Aging on mitochondrial stress enhancing senolytic effectiveness, plus work in Aging Cell on restoring aged natural killer cell function.

title: WHOOP Enters $34.5M ARPA-H-Backed Bid to Quantify Aging
description: Detailed analysis of how the Stanford-WHOOP-Buck Institute coalition will build a composite 'Intrinsic Capacity' aging score, with decentralized trials run through YMCA and OpenCures.

title: ARPA-H Awards Seven Research Teams to Add More Healthy Years to Americans' Lives
description: Official ARPA-H announcement of the PROSPR program awardees, including plans for decentralized in-home trial designs measuring health outcomes from aging in 1-3 years instead of decades.

title: Brown University Wins $22M ARPA-H Award for Censavudine Aging Trial
description: ARPA-H awards up to $22M for a five-year project testing Censavudine in 200+ healthy adults aged 60-65 to determine if the drug can slow human biological aging.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: The longevity biotech market, valued at ~$30.7B in 2026, is projected to reach $52.8B by 2035. Guide covers cellular reprogramming, senolytics, organ regeneration, and AI-driven diagnostics.

title: The $63B Longevity Revolution: How Science Is Rewriting the Rules of Aging
description: The global longevity market ($21.3B in 2024) is projected to reach $63B by 2035. Covers Altos Labs reprogramming, Retro Biosciences pipeline, AI drug discovery, and SGLT2 inhibitor breakthroughs.

title: First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial Cleared by the FDA
description: Life Biosciences receives IND clearance for ER-100, the first-ever human trial of a cellular reprogramming technology targeting age-related eye diseases using Yamanaka factors.

title: Norn Group: Industrializing Longevity with $34M in Research Grants
description: Martin Borch Jensen describes Norn Group's effort to systematically fund longevity bottlenecks, including $34M in Impetus Grants and a clinical trial framework for aging.

title: Stanford's Lloyd Minor: Extending Our Healthy Lives -- What the Science Says
description: Stanford dean covers organ clocks predicting lifespan, two waves of molecular aging (40s and 60s), and super ager research showing immune system biological age as the key to healthy longevity.

title: The First Human Test of a Rejuvenation Method Will Begin Shortly
description: Life Biosciences will test cellular reprogramming in human volunteers for blindness, using partial reprogramming to reset epigenetic code without causing de-differentiation or tumors.

title: Top 10 Longevity and Anti-Aging Breakthroughs of 2025
description: Year in review: PEARL rapamycin trial, GLP-1s as longevity drugs, SGLT2 inhibitors lengthening telomeres, NIA ITP 20-year results confirming rapamycin, and urolithin A cardioprotection.
